title: sorbus umbellata subsp. flabellifolia
intro: Sorbus umbellata subsp. flabellifolia, also known as the mountain ash, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its compound leaves and showy white flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sorbus umbellata subsp. flabellifolia include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antimicrobial agent to combat infections. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves, flowers, or bark, which can be consumed orally or used topically to promote wound healing and skin health.
